hero.name: Nader Lobandi
hero.title: Data Scientist & PhD Researcher
hero.pitch: Data Scientist and PhD researcher at the University of Denver, specializing in deep learning and computer vision. 4+ years of industry experience across healthcare, transportation, and energy. Author of 4 peer-reviewed publications in IEEE Sensors Letters and Biosensors and Bioelectronics.

experience.title: Data Scientist
bullets: Built a cardiovascular risk prediction model using LightGBM and XGBoost, achieving AUC 0.89, with full lifecycle support from data ingestion through hyperparameter tuning to Modal deployment with MLflow tracking.
Implemented real-time voice interaction via Vertex AI STT/TTS and designed 3 agentic workflow types — secure document access, intent routing, and adaptive user support — for an elder-care platform.
Engineered a RAG pipeline over patient documents with persistent chatbot memory and user accounts, reducing repeated data-lookup steps to ~0 per session (~estimated).

experience.title: Data Scientist
bullets: Analyzed terabytes of city-scale traffic data using PySpark, SQL, and Delta Live Tables on Databricks, uncovering that over 70% of streaming feeds originated from a single unreliable vendor.

Deployed an internal RAG chatbot with SQL execution, giving project managers natural-language access to employee hours, assignments, and reporting data in ~seconds vs. multi-day manual lookups (~estimated).
Developed LSTM, Prophet, and SARIMA time-series models to forecast energy demand for NY Transit Authority, improving load-prediction accuracy to support peak-reduction strategies.

experience.title: Data Scientist Co-op
bullets: Built a bilateral filtering preprocessing pipeline for high-dimensional biomedical images, improving cell quantification accuracy by ~20%.
Integrated AWS S3 for scalable, real-time image retrieval, replacing local storage and supporting a ~3-month continuous clinical imaging data workflow.

experience.title: Research Scientist
bullets: Trained LSTM, Gaussian mixture, and regression models on time-series COVID biosensor data, achieving a best detection recall of 95% under noisy measurement conditions.
Published 4 peer-reviewed papers in IEEE Sensors Letters, Biosensors and Bioelectronics, and IEEE CICC on ML-driven biosensor detection systems.
Formulated hypothesis-testing frameworks using MAP, Bayesian, Neyman-Pearson, and Minimax criteria, reducing false-alarm trade-offs across 3+ detection threshold configurations.

gaps: Kubernetes and container orchestration at scale
dbt and modern data transformation tooling
Rust or Go for systems-level programming
Mobile and edge ML deployment (TFLite, CoreML)

projects.name: Financial Time-Series & Document QA
projects.description: Built an educational finance tool to help non-expert users explore forecasting strategies and consume company fundamentals from SEC filings in plain language. Enabled interactive backtesting with reliable forecast horizons up to two months, paired with a chat-based interface for querying 10-K reports.

projects.name: Personal Finance Web App
projects.description: Built a self-hosted alternative to tools like Intuit, giving users full ownership over their financial data while tracking budgets, expenses, and investment goals. Delivered dashboards and reports summarizing financial health, with automated alerts for suspicious transactions and secure multi-user authentication.

projects.name: Financial News Sentiment Analysis
projects.description: Built an NLP system to interpret high-volume financial news and quantify market sentiment, supporting faster and less biased trading-signal extraction. Benchmarked classical and transformer-based approaches across two datasets (Alpha Vantage news, Yahoo Finance), with FinBERT achieving 0.73 accuracy / 0.74 macro-F1 on Alpha Vantage and BERT-uncased reaching 0.86 / 0.86 on Yahoo — validated against real-world ticker movements (e.g., VLN, HSII).

projects.name: E-Waste Sustainability Assistant
projects.description: Built a vision tool that identifies electronic waste and returns its carbon footprint, recoverable value, and a safe disposal action, addressing the growing volume of recyclable e-waste in data centers. Delivered an end-to-end pipeline that turns a photo into a defensible refurbish/recycle/review decision in seconds, backed by a curated knowledge base with full source provenance for every CO₂ and metals figure.

education.institution: University of Denver, Ritchie School of Engineering & Computer Science
education.focus: Deep Learning, Computer Vision, Foundation Models, GPU Computing & CUDA Programming

education.institution: Northeastern University, Khoury College of Computer Sciences
education.honors: Gordon Institute LEADERS Fellowship; LEADERS Leadership Certificate
National Physics Olympiad Silver Medal; Ranked top 0.1% out of 100,000+ applicants in national university entrance exam

meta.siteTitle: Nader Lobandi — Data Scientist & PhD Researcher
meta.siteDescription: Portfolio of Nader Lobandi, a Data Scientist and PhD student at the University of Denver specializing in deep learning, computer vision, and production ML systems — with 4+ years of industry experience and 4 peer-reviewed publications.
meta.tagline: PhD researcher building ML systems that ship.

phd.researchStatement: PhD researcher building ML systems that ship — specializing in deep learning, multimodal models, and vision foundation models. Strong mathematical foundations and systems-level thinking across the full ML lifecycle, from architecture design to production deployment.

milestones.title: PhD Research Day — Poster Presentation
milestones.description: Presented research on Digital Twin systems for Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) at the University of Denver PhD Research Day.
milestones.photoCaption: PhD Research Day — Digital Twin for MCI poster

milestones.title: Summit Hack 26 — 2nd Place
milestones.description: Built an e-waste sustainability assistant using computer vision and RAG to identify electronics and return carbon footprint, recoverable value, and safe disposal guidance.

milestones.title: Joined PhD Program at University of Denver
milestones.description: Began doctoral studies in Computer Science under Dr. Mohammad Mahoor, focusing on deep learning, computer vision, and multimodal foundation models.
